headlines.title: Trump admin appears to back off $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after rare GOP backlash
headlines.description: The Trump administration signaled it is backing off on creating a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund announced by the Justice Department that could send money to allies of President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Primary challengers threaten to unseat House members across the country
headlines.description: Four House members have already lost re-election bids so far this year, just a few months into primary season.

headlines.title: Bernie Sanders defends Graham Platner after reports of sexually explicit text messages
headlines.description: Other progressive members of Congress who endorsed Platner, the leading Democratic candidate for the Senate in Maine, are also coming to his defense.

headlines.title: Democratic Senate candidate faces new scandal
headlines.description: Graham Platner, the Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, is speaking out following his latest controversy over sexually text messages he sent to half a dozen women while married. NBC News' Ryan Nobles reports.

headlines.title: Trump speaks to NBC News amid Iran talks and administration's apparent abandonment of $1.8B fund
headlines.description: President Trump said he would be 'okay' if the Iranian regime refused to return to talks. The administration also appeared to abandon its $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

headlines.title: U.K. bars left-wing influencers from entering country ahead of speaking gigs
headlines.description: The progressive political commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker said they had been barred from entering the United Kingdom to speak at events. Uygur, who co-hosts the YouTube series “The Young Turks,” and his nephew Piker, who broadcasts his commentary on the video platform Twitch, said on social media they had been blocked because of their views about Israel. NBC News' Sahil Kapur reports.

headlines.title: Massive leadership vacuum weakens U.S. diplomacy in the face of global crises
headlines.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Trump admin outlines who would be exempt from Medicaid work requirements
headlines.description: Pregnant women, parents of young children, veterans with disabilities and several other groups will be exempt from Medicaid’s new work requirements, the Trump administration said Monday

headlines.title: Democrats zero in on an anti-corruption message: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Allan Smith and Natasha Korecki explore how Democrats are trying to turn the GOP’s “drain the swamp” message on its head.

headlines.title: Sen. Chuck Schumer lays out plan to fight Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund in Congress
headlines.description: The Senate minority leader said Democrats would use Senate procedure to try to kill President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, forcing Republicans to vote to preserve the compensation money.

headlines.title: 30 gallons of fuel spilled on National Mall after event for America’s 250th birthday
headlines.description: More than 30 gallons of fuel spilled onto the National Mall from generators that were used during the recent Freedom 250 event.
